content.problemStatement:
Roughly speaking, this problem asks you to create the checker of the problem used in December CookOff.

You are given a tree with NNN vertices and a string CCC of length NNN, consisting of the characters `'R'`, `'G'` and `'B'`.

For each integer i (1≤i≤N)i\ (1\leq i\leq N)i (1≤i≤N), CiC_iCi​ is the color of vertex iii — `'R'` is for Red, `'G'` is for Green and `'B'` is for Blue.

The input is called **valid** if for all pairs of Blue vertices (u, v), u≠v(u,\ v),\ u\neq v(u, v), u=v, there is at least one Red vertex and one Green vertex on the simple path between uuu and vvv.

Your task is to validate the input for given TTT test cases. For each test case, print `Yes` if the input is **valid**, otherwise `No`.


Input Format

The first line of input contains one positive integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains one positive integer NNN, the number of vertices of the tree.
The second line of each test case contains a string CCC, denoting the colors of the vertices.
Each of the following N−1N - 1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv, denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv in the tree.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing `Yes` if the input is valid, and `No` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
constraints: The sum of NNN for each test case is at most 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105
CCC consists of three types of characters, `'R'`, `'G'` and `'B'`
The input graph forms a tree

Explanation:
Test case 1:

The only pair is (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4).


Test case 3:

There are no pairs.


Test case 5:

The only pair is (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4). There are no Green vertices on the path between 111 and 444.
